seoTitle: Create a Task List in Microsoft Loop and Sync to Planner
metaDescription: Build a task list in Microsoft Loop and connect it to Microsoft Planner so completed items update automatically in both places.
intro: Manually duplicating tasks between a Loop page and Microsoft Planner wastes time and risks inconsistent status updates. This guide covers building a task list in Loop, assigning owners and due dates, and connecting it to Planner so completions sync automatically between the two.

steps.heading: Deploy the task list block
steps.body: Place your cursor on an empty line in your Loop page and type /task list to drop a fresh action matrix onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Enter task details
steps.body: Type your task name and use the connected calendar picker to set a target due date.

steps.heading: Assign an owner
steps.body: Click the Assigned To column and select your project colleague to allocate ownership of the task.

steps.heading: Connect to Planner
steps.body: Go to the page header panel, click the Task Apps trigger, and select Open in Planner to spin up a connected cloud plan.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Check the live cloud connection inside your browser window to confirm the sync pipeline is active.

steps.heading: Test the two-way sync
steps.body: Check the tick mark box inside Planner and confirm the status auto-updates back into your Loop page.

faq.question: Does the sync work in both directions between Loop and Planner?
faq.answer: Yes, marking a task complete in Planner is designed to update its status back on the Loop page automatically.

faq.question: Do I need to create the Planner plan separately first?
faq.answer: No, using the Task Apps trigger from the Loop page header can spin up the connected Planner plan for you.

faq.question: Can I change task ownership after the sync is set up?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update the Assigned To field at any time, though it's worth verifying the sync still reflects the new owner correctly.

faq.question: What if the sync doesn't activate after clicking Open in Planner?
faq.answer: Give it a moment to establish the cloud connection, and if it still doesn't sync, try refreshing the page or checking your account's Planner access.
